pattern: Track two running lengths — one ending on an up-step, one on a down-step — and extend whichever matches the current comparison while resetting the other, a sliding flip-flop in one pass.
hints: A turbulent run alternates strictly between rising and falling adjacent pairs, so equal neighbours always break the run.
At each adjacent pair you only need to know whether the previous step went up or down to decide if the current one continues the alternation.
Keep up and down lengths: if nums[i] > nums[i-1] set up = down + 1 and down = 1; if less, down = up + 1 and up = 1; if equal reset both to 1. The answer is the max of these over all i (at least 1).

edge_inputs.description: long alternating run in the middle
strictly increasing has no turbulence beyond length 2
two elements forming a single step
plateau in the middle resets the count

stress.description: small value range so plateaus and flips both appear often
